title.en: Asking for more side dishes at a Korean restaurant
title.ko: 이것 좀 더 주실 수 있어요? (식당에서 반찬 요청하기)

description.en: Love Korean side dishes? Learn the polite way to ask for a refill and the unwritten etiquette of "Banchan culture" in Korea.
description.ko: 한국 식당의 매력인 반찬 리필! 하지만 무조건 많이 먹는 게 정답은 아니죠. 사장님을 배려하며 기분 좋게 리필을 요청하는 매너와 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Food. Restaurant. Request. Manners. Side dishes. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 입맛을 다시며 흥분된 목소리로 바로 시작
content.text: Mina, I’m at this amazing Galbi restaurant, and these side dishes are out of this world!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I’ve already finished the Kimchi and this potato salad. Can I really just ask for more for free?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럼요! 한국 식당의 가장 큰 매력이 바로 반찬 리필 이잖아요.
content.translation: Haha, of course! The biggest charm of Korean restaurants is this 'Banchan refill'.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 하지만 꼭 기억해야 할 매너가 하나 있어요.
content.translation: But there's one 'Manner' you must remember.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh? Is there a limit?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 보통 한국 사람들은 정말 필요한 만큼만 대개 한두 번 정도만 리필을 해요.
content.translation: Usually, Koreans only refill as much as they really need, typically once or twice.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 사장님들도 이익을 남기셔야 하니까 서로 배려하는 마음이 중요하거든요.
content.translation: Since the owners also need to make a profit, a heart of mutual consideration is important.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Ah, I see! It’s about respect, not just eating as much as I can.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 오늘은 사장님 기분도 좋게 만드는 매너 있는 리필의 리액션을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Right! Today, we'll learn 'mannered refill' reactions that also make the owner feel good!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이것 좀 더 주실 수 있어요? Adding "좀" makes it much softer, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! "할 수 있냐" 고 물어보는 방식이라서 훨씬 정중하게 들려요.
content.translation: Yes! Since it's a way of asking "if they can," it sounds much more polite.

insight.title: 이것 좀 더 주실 수 있어요?
insight.description: 'Can I have some more of this?'
insight.usage_tip: '이것(This)' + '좀(A bit/Please)' + '더(More)' + '주실 수 있어요(Can you give)'. The '좀' is essential for sounding natural and showing respect for the staff's busy schedule.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 사장님. 이것 좀 더 주실 수 있어요?
content.translation: Boss, could I have some more of this?

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if the staff looks really busy?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "죄송한데 반찬 조금만 더 주실 수 있나요?" 라고 해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "죄송한데, 반찬 좀만 더 주실 수 있나요?".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "죄송한데" 를 앞에 붙이면 바쁜 사장님을 배려하는 느낌이 확 살아나요.
content.translation: Adding "Sorry, but..." at the beginning really brings out the feeling of considering the busy owner.

insight.title: 죄송한데 ~ 좀만 더 주실 수 있나요?
insight.description: 'I'm sorry, but could I have just a bit more?'
insight.usage_tip: '죄송한데(I'm sorry but)' + '좀만(Just a bit)'. Adding '만' to '좀' suggests you aren't asking for a huge amount.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm at a busy Kimchi Jjigae place. I've finished my favorite side dish, but the staff looks a bit rushed.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기요, 사장님! 바쁘신데 죄송한데요.
content.translation: Excuse me, Boss! I'm sorry to bother you while you're busy.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아, 네! 뭐 더 드릴까요?
content.translation: Ah, yes! Shall I give you more of something?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이 어묵볶음이 너무 맛있어서요. 좀만 더 주실 수 있어요?
content.translation: Because this stir-fried fish cake is so delicious. Could I have just a bit more?

insight.title: 좀만 더
insight.usage_tip: This shows the owner that you are mindful of not wasting food.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아이구, 맛있게 드셔주니 제가 더 고맙죠! 많이 드릴게요!
content.translation: Oh, I'm more thankful since you're eating it so well! I'll give you plenty!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아뇨. 남기면 안 되니까 조금만 더 주셔도 돼요. 감사합니다!
content.translation: No, I shouldn't leave any, so you can just give me a little more. Thank you!

insight.title: 남기면 안 되니까
insight.usage_tip: This is the ultimate "manner" phrase in Korea. It shows you respect the food and the owner's hard work.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 매너가 좋으시네요! 여기 있습니다!
content.translation: John, you really have great manners! Here you go!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, I feel so much better knowing that I’m being a responsible customer!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국에서 리필은 권리라기보다는 서로 주고받는 정 이거든요.
content.translation: Right. In Korea, refilling is less of a 'right' and more about 'Jeong' shared between people.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "mannered refill" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이것 좀 더 주실 수 있어요?
content.translation: Could I have some more of this?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 바쁘신데 죄송하지만 조금만 더 주세요!
content.translation: I'm sorry to bother you, but please give me a little more!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너무 맛있어서 더 먹고 싶네요!
content.translation: It's so delicious I want to eat more!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 남기면 안 되니까 조금만 더 주세요!
content.translation: Since I shouldn't leave any, just a little more please!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잘 먹었습니다!
content.translation: I ate well! / Thank you for the meal!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 식당에서 리필을 요청할 때 사장님의 마음까지 생각하는 따뜻한 리액션을 건네보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, when you ask for a refill at a restaurant today, try offering a warm reaction that considers the owner's heart too.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 타인을 배려하는 마음이 성숙한 문화를 만들어요. (A heart that considers others creates a mature culture.)
모든 가게는 정당한 이익을 남겨야 운영될 수 있어요. (Every store needs to make a fair profit to operate.)
어려운 부탁일수록 정중하게 말하는 것이 예의예요. (The more difficult the request, the more important it is to speak politely.)
음식을 남기지 않는 것은 요리사에 대한 예의예요. (Not leaving food behind is good manners toward the chef.)
너무 좋아하면 얼굴에 다 티가 나요. (If you like it too much, it all shows on your face.)
시간이 늦었으니 이만 들어가 볼게요. (Since it's late, I'll be heading out now.)
